name: Baltic Sea Region
climate: Maritime to humid continental; cold winters with sea ice in the north and east; cool summers; moderate precipitation; limited daylight in winter

neighbors.name: Nordic Council
neighbors.relevance: Sweden, Finland, Denmark are both Nordic and Baltic littoral states; shared defense and energy interests

neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: All Baltic littoral states except Russia are EU members; EU funds Baltic energy and transport infrastructure

neighbors.name: Commonwealth of Independent States
neighbors.relevance: Russia's Baltic coastline and Kaliningrad exclave create direct security friction

neighbors.name: Visegrád Group
neighbors.relevance: Poland bridges Baltic and V4 regions; Suwalki Gap is a shared NATO concern

neighbors.name: Ruhr Area
neighbors.relevance: German Baltic ports (Rostock, Lubeck) connect to Ruhr industrial hinterland

neighbors.name: Arctic Sea Route
neighbors.relevance: Baltic ports serve as staging points for Arctic shipping routes via the North Sea

links.description: Nordic states dominate the western and northern Baltic; joint maritime surveillance and energy grids
EU Baltic Energy Market Interconnection Plan links Baltic states to European energy networks
NATO-Russia confrontation zone; Kaliningrad militarization and hybrid warfare threats
Poland anchors both V4 and Baltic NATO defense; joint military exercises on the eastern flank
Baltic Sea ports funnel raw materials and containerized goods to German industrial centers
Ukrainian and Romanian grain transits from Black Sea to Baltic ports via inland waterways and rail
